issues: The summary is almost entirely a process log narrating failed attempts (clicked X, tried Y, didn't work) rather than results. It fails to identify that the actual task goal is unknown - we don't know what the user wants to accomplish. The 'Remaining Work: None' is incorrect; the task is stuck but not complete. Key facts missing: what the actual goal is, whether Silver plans exist on the page, whether filters were successfully applied. The 'Current State' mentions Bronze plan but doesn't clarify if this is filtered or unfiltered results.

Fact Retention: The summary incorrectly states both Chromium-browser and Mozilla Firefox are open, when only Firefox is being used. It also incorrectly states the agent 'clicked on the Continue button' multiple times when the page never actually advanced - the agent was stuck on the same page throughout. The 'Completed Work' section is bloated with failed attempts. Process Suppression: Severely violated - the summary reads like a detailed click log with every single action narrated ('Clicked on...', 'Scrolled up to verify...', 'Clicked on...'). Current State: Vague - says 'Tell us about you' page is active but doesn't clarify the agent is STUCK on this page and unable to proceed. Remaining Work: Reasonably accurate about what needs to be done, though it doesn't acknowledge the technical problem preventing progress.

Multiple factual errors: (1) Used Epiphany browser, not Chromium; (2) Current state is wrong - the agent is actually on the 'Are you married?' page, not 'Who's in your household?' page; (3) Process suppression is weak - mentions 'Navigated to', 'Entered', 'Selected' which are process steps; (4) Missing key detail that 'Yes' was selected for married status but the page didn't advance. The remaining work is reasonably accurate but could be more specific about needing to troubleshoot why the Continue button didn't work.

The summary preserves most price data accurately but has issues: 1) The 'Current State' section is cut off/incomplete in the output. 2) The 'Remaining Work' section is missing entirely - based on the conversation, the agent was about to search for Crucial P5 1TB but didn't complete it, so remaining work should be noted. 3) The URLs in Key Data appear fabricated/concocted - they don't match the actual URLs from the conversation and contain made-up query parameters like '_nkw=Samsung+980+Pro+1TB+Sold+Prices' which never appeared in the actual eBay URLs. 4) The summary mentions Crucial P5 in Key Data URLs but no actual price data was collected for it (search was started but not completed).

Fact Retention: Missing the specific return flight detail (7:45 PM departure, 10:48 PM arrival) that caused the price jump; also doesn't mention MDW search was abandoned/focus shifted to ORD only. Process Suppression: Contains excessive step-by-step narration ('excluded Frontier and Spirit', 'set outbound departure time', 'viewed complete round-trip details') that reads like a click log rather than focusing on results.

Major issues: (1) Fact Retention - The summary completely omits the detailed Silver plan data that was collected: Plan 1 (Sendero Health Capital Silver, $581.27/month, $6,000 deductible, $8,900 OOP max) and Plan 2 (Sendero Health Original Silver, $597.59/month, $4,250 deductible, $7,750 OOP max). These are key findings that should be preserved. (2) Process Suppression - The summary extensively narrates every click and scroll action ('Scrolled up and down', 'Clicked on', 'Went back') rather than focusing on results. (3) Remaining Work - The listed remaining work is largely incorrect/outdated; the conversation shows the user already entered household info and is now comparing Silver plans, not needing to 'enter age 33 for second person' or 'enter income'. The actual remaining work should be to view and document the third Silver plan details. (4) Current State - Vague description; should specify that two Silver plans have been documented and the agent is positioned to view the third.

Summary focuses heavily on process steps (searched, found, saved) rather than actual facts discovered. Missing key technical details found: nvidia-smi command syntax, RTX 3090 power specs (350W default, 100W min, 400W max), persistence daemon purpose. 'Remaining Work' is accurate but vague. The summary falsely claims all 41 tasks are complete when only Tests 1-4 (31 tasks) were actually finished. The summary hallucinates '7/7 ✓' for Test 4 and claims 'None' remaining work, which is completely wrong. The conversation also shows Tests 5+ were never started. Process suppression is mediocre - while it avoids some click logs, it still includes excessive navigation details rather than pure facts.

Process suppression is poor - the summary extensively narrates steps taken (clicked X, scrolled, tried Y, closed window, focused window) rather than focusing on results. The summary contains fabricated price data and sales quantities. The conversation shows the agent browsing eBay sold listings for 1TB and 2TB NVMe SSDs across Samsung, Western Digital, Crucial, and SK Hynix brands, but NO actual prices or unit counts are visible in the conversation - the agent never extracted or reported specific dollar amounts or quantities sold. The prices ($120, $115, $105, $100 for 1TB; $250, $240, $230, $220 for 2TB) and unit counts appear to be hallucinated. The agent did navigate to all the correct brand-filtered pages for both 1TB and 2TB searches, so the structure of work completed is accurate, but the specific data points are invented.

The summary is deeply flawed.
